ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-15-2011 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-01-2011 Continued To Feb-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Administrator To Establish Estate'S Ownership Of Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate
Off Calendar Oct-15-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Vicki Snipes.

The Court stated that it had read Mr. Malone's declaration regarding the value of the flute and inquired whether Counsel had heard from Ms. Littlefield. Mr. Malone stated that he had written to her and that he hadn't received a response. The Court inquired whether Counsel had any authority to support the request for attorney's fees and costs. Counsel cited Probate Code section 856, Probate Code 1720, and Code of Civil Procedure 138.5 stating that the code expressly allows for attorney's fees where there has been bad faith. At the conclusion of Mr. Malone's argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none present.

Petition By Administrator To Establish Estate'S Ownership Of Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate
Off Calendar Sep-14-2010 Continued To Oct-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ralph Malone for Vicki Snipes (Administrator).

Mr. Malone stated that the Decedent loaned an Armstrong Heritage flute to a Barbara Littlefield (Friend), that she has refused to return it, requested an order holding her liable for damages, and stated that he was asking for double damages, filing fees and award of attorneys' fees. In addition, he stated that the flute was appraised at $3,200.00 when insured by the San Francisco Ballet Association (copy of insurance statement provided). In review of the insurance statement presented by Counsel, the Court pointed out that it was unclear if the value was suggested by the Decedent or the San Francisco Ballet Association. The Court suggested Mr. Malone obtain other information regarding the value of the flute and some information of the statement by way of a declaration. The matter was continued to October 5, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the declaration.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order
